title: refactor: remove modelId from model registry
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Removes the `modelId` field from `ModelDefinition` and all registry entries (text, image, audio, embeddings, 3D, realtime, community). It was unused metadata for most categories and duplicated the registry key for image/video.
- Keeps load-bearing upstream IDs in Gen routing, matching how text routing already works: audio IDs live beside the ElevenLabs and Qwen provider handlers, embedding IDs live in the embedding dispatcher, and Azure deployment names live beside the realtime connector.
- Splits audio routing by provider. ElevenLabs and Qwen maps derive exact key unions, single-model music/SFX IDs stay inside their handlers, and dispatch no longer uses a mixed `Partial` lookup with casts and `undefined` handling.
- Perplexity billing adjustment logs now report the registry model name instead of the upstream ID (`calculateBillingAdjustments` gains a `model` parameter).
- VCR snapshot hashing uses `provider` only; affected Portkey snapshots were re-recorded.
- Drops the `ModelId` union and per-category `*ModelId` type exports; `ModelDefinition` is no longer generic.

Unblocks #12495: the canonical rename kept `modelId` only because this routing cleanup was pending.

gist.headline: The registry sheds a skin
gist.blurb: The model registry put down some duplicate labels and let each provider keep its own directions. Fewer casts, fewer mystery IDs, and a tidier garden of routes.
gist.summary: Removed the unused `modelId` field and related ID type exports from every model registry category, keeping provider-specific upstream IDs in Gen audio, embedding, and realtime routing handlers instead.
gist.impact: Developers get simpler, more type-safe model routing: ElevenLabs and Qwen audio maps now use exact key unions, billing logs use registry model names, and VCR snapshots hash by provider only.
gist.visual_concept: Depict a cozy registry garden where duplicate model-ID tags have been removed from labeled plant pots, while small provider-specific route signs sit neatly beside their own plants. Polly acts as the observant gardener with a clipboard, organizing paths between an audio flower, embedding sprouts, and a realtime connector vine; the scene represents routing logic moving to the places where it is actually used.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly, the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot with black antennae, oval eyes, pink rectangular blush, a small smile, and light blue rounded wings, wearing a tiny gardener visor and holding a clipboard in a code-garden; she removes duplicate tags from lime green (#ecf874) model registry pots while tidy signposts route toward audio flowers, embedding sprouts, and a realtime vine beside a softly glowing retro terminal. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel mint-lavender-peach gradient background, chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley or A Short Hike, subtle CRT glow, nostalgic but beautiful.
